perturbation.original_text: Licensee agrees that its right of preemption shall not be exercised in an arbitrary or unreasonable manner, or for commercial advantage.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may exercise its right of preemption as it deems necessary.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that restricts Licensee's preemption rights to being non-arbitrary, non-unreasonable, and not for commercial advantage, the modified text creates a scenario where Licensee can preempt WLIB Programs for any reason, including commercial gain. This contradicts the spirit of the original agreement where preemption should not be for commercial advantage. This will also create conflict with Section 3 that states: During the Term, Licensee shall broadcast on the HD2 Channel the WLIB Programs delivered by Programmer during the Broadcasting Period specified in Section 2 above, subject to the provisions of Section 6 below.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Licensee shall broadcast on the HD2 Channel the WLIB Programs delivered by Programmer during the Broadcasting Period specified in Section 2 above, subject to the provisions of Section 6 below.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the licensee's right of preemption to situations that were not arbitrary, unreasonable, or for commercial advantage. The changed text removes these limitations, allowing the licensee to exercise its right of preemption as it deems necessary, even for commercial advantage. This directly contradicts the section stating the Licensee shall broadcast WLIB Programs. The unlimited preemption right effectively nullifies the obligation to broadcast those programs, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the licensee can now preempt the programs for any reason, contradicting the original intent and the section requiring them to broadcast the program. This falls under the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, and in the absence of a timely cure pursuant to Section 11.3, the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement, effective immediately upon written notice to the defaulting party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'and in the absence of a timely cure pursuant to Section 11.3' and effective immediately upon written notice to the defaulting party, the modified text allows for immediate termination without a cure period, directly contradicting Section 11.3 which specifies a 15-day cure period. This creates uncertainty about the conditions required before the agreement can be terminated. This will also create a contradiction with Section 11.3: 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, any Event of Default will not be deemed to have occurred until fifteen (15) days after the non-defaulting party has provided the defaulting party with written notice specifying the Event of Default and such Event of Default remains uncured.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, any Event of Default will not be deemed to have occurred until fifteen (15) days after the non-defaulting party has provided the defaulting party with written notice specifying the Event of Default and such Event of Default remains uncured.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause specifying a cure period, directly conflicting with the contradicted text which explicitly states that an Event of Default is not deemed to have occurred until fifteen days after written notice and remains uncured. This creates a contradiction regarding the conditions required before the agreement can be terminated.
